description: 修复反向拖拽绘制和橡皮擦光标跟随的致命 Bug，重构以视口中心为基准的缩放引擎，引入全新的对象层级（Z-Index）图层管理系统。

userStories.title: Fix reverse-direction drawing with immutable origin coordinates
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw shapes by dragging in any direction (including left and up), so that the shape always appears correctly and never disappears regardless of drag direction.
acceptanceCriteria: originX and originY are captured exactly once in pointerdown and are NEVER updated during pointermove or pointerup

During pointermove, closed shape draft element bounding box uses: x = Math.min(originX, currentX), y = Math.min(originY, currentY), width = Math.abs(currentX - originX), height = Math.abs(currentY - originY)
Rectangle tool: dragging from (500,500) to (200,200) leaves a visible 300x300 rectangle at (200,200)
Rhombus tool: same coordinate normalization, shape renders correctly in all 4 drag directions

CRITICAL: Do NOT apply this absolute Math.min/Math.abs normalization to directional vector tools (Line, Arrow, Freehand). They must preserve their original mathematical start/end point directionality.
SceneElement width and height for closed shapes are NEVER negative
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix eraser cursor not following mouse during drag
userStories.description: As a user, I want the eraser cursor icon to follow my mouse movement while dragging, so I always know where the eraser is positioned.
acceptanceCriteria: When activeTool === 'eraser', canvas container applies cursor: url(eraser-icon), auto !important at all times including :active state
pointerdown on canvas calls e.preventDefault() to suppress native text selection and drag-and-drop without breaking touch
Eraser icon follows the mouse pixel-accurately during continuous drag at any speed
Cursor does not freeze, flicker, or disappear at any point during the drag
Releasing the mouse does not leave the eraser cursor stuck; it persists while eraser tool is active
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement viewport-center-based zoom algorithm
userStories.description: As a user, I want zoom in/out to keep the center of my viewport anchored to the same world coordinates, so that my view does not fly away when I click the plus or minus buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Zoom controller computes viewport center: viewportCenterX = viewportWidth / 2, viewportCenterY = viewportHeight / 2
Converts viewport center to world coordinates: worldX = (viewportCenterX - scrollX) / zoom, worldY = (viewportCenterY - scrollY) / zoom
After updating zoom, recalculates scroll to compensate: newScrollX = viewportCenterX - (worldX * newZoom), newScrollY = viewportCenterY - (worldY * newZoom)
newZoom, newScrollX, newScrollY are applied atomically to AppState
Drawing a square at viewport center, then repeatedly clicking + to zoom in, the square remains visually centered at every zoom step
Repeatedly clicking - to zoom out, the square also remains centered
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement layer management core array logic with Undo/Redo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the layer ordering logic to correctly reorder elements in the Scene.elements array and push a history snapshot after each operation, so that Undo/Redo works correctly after layer changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Bring to Front: selected element is splice-removed and push-appended to the end of the elements array
Send to Back: selected element is splice-removed and unshift-inserted at index 0
Bring Forward: selected element at index i is swapped with element at index i+1
Send Backward: selected element at index i is swapped with element at index i-1
Each of the 4 operations triggers a full canvas redraw after the array mutation
Each of the 4 operations pushes a complete state snapshot to the Undo/Redo history stack in the same format as existing history
Undo after a layer operation correctly restores the previous element ordering
Redo after undoing a layer operation correctly re-applies the ordering change
Operations only apply when exactly ONE element is selected; multi-selection is a no-op
Export 4 pure utility functions: bringToFront, sendToBack, bringForward, sendBackward -- each returns the mutated array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Layer management toolbar UI with button state machine
userStories.description: As a user, I want 4 layer control buttons in the toolbar so I can adjust the stacking order of a selected shape with a single click.
acceptanceCriteria: New Layer button group added to toolbar second row between Style and Save sections, visually separated by | dividers
Region labeled with title text Layer
4 icon buttons: Bring Forward, Send Backward, Bring to Front, Send to Back
All 4 buttons disabled when selectedElementIds is empty, when multiple elements are selected, OR when a Group is selected (strictly single un-grouped element allowed)
Send Backward and Send to Back are disabled when the selected element is the FIRST visible (isDeleted: false) element in the array
Bring Forward and Bring to Front are disabled when the selected element is the LAST visible (isDeleted: false) element in the array
Clicking an enabled button calls the corresponding US-004 utility function and triggers redraw and history pipeline
Disabled buttons have reduced opacity; enabled buttons show hover and active states
Typecheck passes
